name: Accessibility Framework

ux_decisions.decision: Treat accessibility as a baseline requirement for Stage 5, not an optional polish pass.
ux_decisions.rationale: Core workflows include forms, status changes, financial context, uploads, and permission boundaries.

keyboard_requirements: All navigation, forms, tabs, modals, uploads, menus, and admin actions must be keyboard reachable.
Focus order must follow visible layout and scope hierarchy.
Modal confirmations for public promotion and provider handoff must trap focus and return focus to triggering control.
screen_reader_requirements: Scope banners must be announced as contextual status.
Async receipt/provider status changes must use appropriate live regions.
Receipt original, extracted, translated, and display currency values must have explicit labels.
Permission-denied states must explain access boundary without exposing private data.
form_requirements: Validation errors must be associated with fields.
Currency, amount, paid-by, participant, and visibility controls require clear required/optional state.
External provider URL/reference validation must describe the expected input.
visual_requirements: Do not rely on color alone to distinguish public/private/event scopes.
Use readable contrast for financial and status data.
Use persistent labels for original versus derived receipt values.
Use clear icon plus text for high-risk public exposure actions.
motion_and_timing_requirements: No critical information should depend on animation.
Async status updates must remain visible after transitions.
Timeouts must not discard unsaved user input.
error_messaging_requirements: Errors must state what failed, whether data was saved, and the next recovery action.
Provider handoff errors must clarify that the external provider owns checkout/payment handling.
Receipt processing failures must offer retry or manual entry.

accessibility_risks.risk: Scope and privacy state could be communicated visually only.
accessibility_risks.mitigation: Expose scope text, ARIA labels, and consistent headings for public/private/event contexts.

accessibility_risks.risk: Async receipt/provider updates may not be announced to assistive technology.
accessibility_risks.mitigation: Use live regions and persistent status summaries.

accessibility_risks.risk: Financial and receipt tables may be difficult to navigate.
accessibility_risks.mitigation: Use semantic tables or structured list alternatives with clear headers and captions.

stage_5_handoff_notes: Stage 5 tickets should include keyboard, screen reader, contrast, focus management, and error association acceptance criteria.
